description.value: Fully-managed, serverless NoSQL document database from Google Cloud and Firebase with real-time sync, offline support, and automatic multi-region replication

license.comment: Firestore itself is a closed-source, commercial Google Cloud service. The client SDKs are open source (Apache 2.0) but the database engine is proprietary.

first-release.comment: Cloud Firestore was launched in beta on October 3, 2017.

latest-version.comment: Firestore is a fully-managed cloud service with continuous delivery; there is no user-facing version number. Editions (Standard / Enterprise) exist but are not versioned releases.

actively-maintained.comment: Actively developed; ongoing feature releases including the Enterprise edition and new query engine. Checked 2026-06-09.

database-type.comment: Primary database model is document store.

query-language.comment: No SQL support. Queries are issued via client SDKs, REST and gRPC APIs. Enterprise edition adds optional MongoDB-compatible operations, but the core interface is API/SDK only.

acid-compliant.comment: Supports ACID transactions with strong consistency across multi-region replication.

schema-model.comment: Schema-free; documents can contain arbitrary fields and nested objects.

horizontal-scaling.comment: Built from the ground up to automatically scale horizontally; sharding and distribution are fully managed by Google.

replication.comment: Automatic multi-region replication with strong consistency; DB-Engines classifies this as multi-source replication.

clustering.comment: As a managed service, data distribution and clustering are built-in and automatic, requiring no third-party tools.

cloud-offering.comment: Firestore is itself a fully-managed cloud service; there is no self-hosted production deployment (only a local emulator for development).

full-text-search.comment: Native full-text search is available in the Enterprise edition; Standard edition historically required external solutions such as Algolia or Elasticsearch.

json-support.comment: Document database storing JSON/BSON-compatible documents natively, fully queryable.

geospatial.comment: Native geospatial queries are supported in the Enterprise edition.

time-series.comment: No specialized time-series data type or handling.

stored-procedures.comment: No native server-side stored procedures; server-side logic is implemented externally via Cloud Functions / Cloud Run functions.

triggers.comment: No database-internal triggers; document-change events are handled externally by Cloud Functions / Eventarc rather than by the database engine itself.

views.comment: No support for database views (regular or materialized).

official-gui.comment: Data can be browsed and edited via the Firebase Console, Firestore Studio, and the Google Cloud Console.

cli-tool.comment: Managed via the Firebase CLI and the gcloud CLI.

docker-image.comment: Firestore is a hosted service with no official database image on Docker Hub; only a local emulator (via gcloud / Firebase Emulator Suite) is provided for development.

primary-language.comment: Implementation language is not publicly disclosed; Firestore is a proprietary, closed-source Google Cloud service.

github-stars.comment: Closed-source database with no primary GitHub repository; per RESEARCH.md guidelines, null is used for closed-source databases.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Could not reliably determine the question count for the google-cloud-firestore tag; Stack Overflow tag pages were not accessible during this pass.

db-engines-rank.comment: Overall DB-Engines rank #45 (and #7 among document stores). Ranking changes monthly; checked 2026-06-09.

learning-curve.comment: Gentle for common app use cases: simple document model, excellent documentation and SDKs. Slightly tempered by the lack of SQL and by query/indexing constraints that surprise newcomers.
